steps.title: Spot the participial modifier
steps.content: The phrase “by helping the Tuvan Republic and other Soviet republics established along ethnolinguistic lines assert their prerogatives” is a participial modifier of “reforms” — it explains the circumstances but is not the main action. The sentence still needs its main verb.

steps.title: Select the finite verb for the main clause
steps.content: ”Contributed” is the finite verb that completes the independent clause — the sentence reads “reforms contributed to the Soviet Union's breakup.” The other choices don't work: “contributing” and “having contributed” are non-finite participles that can't serve as the main verb, while “which contributed” creates a relative clause in an unsuitable position. That matches choice A — A is correct.
